hero.title: Visualize, edit and deploy Dynamo rules with context.
hero.copy: A single panel to browse tables by environment, validate sensitive changes and run rule conversions without leaving the web UI.

environment.title: Environments
environment.subtitle: Switch between desa, pre and pro with a clear view of operational risk.

descriptions.desa: Fast iteration with local fallbacks and seed data.

summary.title: Summary
summary.subtitle: Quick snapshot of the active environment.

tables.title: Table inventory
tables.subtitle: Check volume, keys and last update before touching data.

tablesPage.title: Table explorer
tablesPage.subtitle: Select a table, inspect items and edit JSON with environment-based confirmations.
tablesPage.noTablesHelp: No tables were found for this environment. Check credentials/settings or switch environment.
tablesPage.empty: There are no items in this table yet.
tablesPage.editorSubtitle: Editing existing item. You can modify everything except the primary key.
tablesPage.editorSubtitleNew: New item. Fill in the fields and save to create it.
tablesPage.editorSubtitleDuplicate: Duplicate pending save. Change the id if needed and save.
tablesPage.modalSubtitle: Edit the expression, encode it and apply it to the editor JSON.
tablesPage.readonlyBanner: Environment {{env}} is in read-only mode. Changes are disabled.
tablesPage.filterEmpty: No items match the current filter.
tablesPage.editorEmpty: Select an item from the list to edit it.

converter.title: Converter and reverse converter
converter.subtitle: Turn rule expressions into Dynamo JSON and back into readable text for functional reviews.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Set the initial environment, the default table and write permissions per environment.
settings.readonlySubtitle: Check the environments where you want to disable writing and deleting.
settings.importEnvsEmpty: The file contains no valid sections.
settings.envConfigSubtitle: Set the dropdown display name, write access and AWS credentials for each environment.
settings.noEnvsConfigured: No environments configured. Click "+ Add environment" to create the first one.
settings.noEnvSelected: Configure at least one environment below to be able to select it.
settings.envDeleteConfirm: Delete environment "{{name}}"? Its credentials will also be removed.
settings.envNameRequired: Enter a name for the environment before saving.
settings.globalCredsSubtitle: Only needed for remote server access. Stored in the browser. Not needed when running locally with ~/.aws/credentials.
settings.feedbackCredsRequired: Access Key ID and Secret are required.
